data.title: Aaron
data.definition: Aaron is a Hebrew male name that means 'exalted'. There are interpretations of the name that suggest the meaning is 'teacher' or 'mountain of strength'. This is a great name for a pet that is strong in mind and in build.

data.title: Abby
data.definition: Originally a Hebrew name with biblical origins, Abby means 'father's joy'. Perfect for any daddy's girls!

data.title: Abu
data.definition:
The name Abu is a male name of African origin that means 'Nobility'. It is also often used as a nickname as it can also mean 'father of' in Indian culture.

The name Abu is most famous as the mischevious pet monkey of Aladdin!

data.title: Ace
data.definition: As confident a name as a person might be with one of these cards up their sleeve, Ace is actually of Latin origin and means 'One' or 'Expert'. It's a great short, sharp name for the special 'one' in your life.

data.title: Achilles
data.definition: The hero of Homer’s Iliad, Achilles was a warrior known for having a weakness in his heel which eventually caused his demise. Although the name itself comes from the Greek word meaning 'pain', we'd rather focus on the namesake's legendary strength and bravery!

data.title: Acorn
data.definition: The name is derived from the German word 'eichorn', which is a place where squirrels are in abundance. Acorn might make a lovely name for dogs with a brown coat, or little dogs!

data.title: Ada
data.definition: Ada has its origin in German culture and means ‘happiness’ and ‘noble’. The name originally was a shortened version of the name Adelaide.

data.title: Addie
data.definition: Meaning 'noble' in both its original Latin and German forms, Addie can also mean 'sweet'.

data.title: Aero
data.definition: Whilst we might immediately think of the airy and bubbly chocolate bar, Aero is actually a name of Greek origin and means 'of the sky'.

data.title: Aether
data.definition: Aether means brightness and light in Ancient Greek, and according to medieval science is also considered to be the 5th element of alchemical chemistry - the stuff of stars!

data.title: Andromeda
data.definition: This female name is of Greek origin, and means 'leader of humankind' - so not a name for the faint-hearted! For Greek mythology lovers, you will know that Andromeda was the daughter of Cassiopeia, who much like her mother, became a constellation. Andromeda is also the name of the nearest major galaxy to the Milky Way; an unusual name for the star in your life!

data.title: Apollo
data.definition: Apollo is one of the most noted names of Greek mythology and although it means 'destroyer' (and lets face it, what puppy hasn't taken apart a sofa leg or two?), he was also known as the God of the sun, music, poetry and medicine.

data.title: Bolt
data.definition: Ready to hear that sonic bark? Bolt is a famous dog name from the Disney film Bolt. The name Bolt means 'lightning' and 'flash'. So, if you have a pup that runs at the speed of light, this is the name for you!

data.title: Caesar
data.definition: Of course Caesar is best known as arguably the most famous Roman Emperor, but did you know the name actually means 'thick head of hair'? Making it a great name for both pups who are natural-born leaders, or those who are just really, really fluffy!

data.title: Daisy
data.definition:
The happiest of little flowers, Daisy means 'Day's Eye' because it opens its petals when the sun rises, and is as fresh, energetic and sunny as it's namesake suggests.

 

As the name of fashionable and fierce Daisy Duck, this is also a great name for any avid Disney fan (or owner of a sassy pup!)

data.title: Echo
data.definition: The story of Echo is a sad one, as she was a magical wood nymph whose unrequited love for Narcisses eventually caused her to fade to nothing except the shadows of her voice. Don't let the tragic history put you off what is a pretty-sounding name and fantastic for dogs whose job is to listen, like shepherding breeds Rough Collie, Shetland Sheepdog and German Shepherd.

data.title: Finn
data.definition: Finn may be associated with the surname from the classic 'Adventures of Huckleberry Finn' by Mark Twain, but this name has a little more history behind it. The name Finn is originally of Irish decent, and was name of an Irish mythical warrior in folk law history, known to be courageous, intelligent, and brave. We think that Finn is the perfect name for those protectors in your life, whether they are little, or large.

data.title: Gia
data.definition: A shorter form of Gianna, this Italian name means 'God's gracious gift'.

data.title: Henry
data.definition: Henry, is one of the more common French male names. It is derived from the French name Henri, meaning 'house ruler'. Great for a pup that rules your household! The name has also had many famous owners throughout history, from our own King Henry, (well 8 to be exact!) and the formidable batman actor, Henry Cavil.

data.title: Iris
data.definition: Best known as the beautiful Spring blossom, Iris comes from the Greek meaning 'rainbow' and was also the Goddess of the same who road her colourful bridge between heaven and earth. This connection to colour is also how the pigmented part of the eye came to be named!

data.title: Jasper
data.definition: Jasper can variously mean 'treasurer' or 'treasure-bearer' and is a great name for pups who can't help but bring back wonderous treasures to present you with; just make sure you show your appreciation before delicately disposing!

data.title: Luna
data.definition: This dreamy name comes from the Italian meaning 'moon', and was also the name of the Roman Goddess of the Moon. You might also know it from the quirky Harry Potter chracter, Luna Lovegood. This dog name topped the 2021 charts for most popular dog names in the UK.

data.title: Milo
data.definition: Milo is one of our most popular male names, and is said to be derived from many origins. In Slavic languages, 'mil' means 'dear' or 'beloved'. However, there are those that believe the name is derived from the Latin word 'miles' which means 'soldier'. Whichever definition you choose for Milo, this is a sweet, and warm name for your pup.

data.title: Nala
data.definition: Nala has many meanings and is commonly used across many African cultures, from 'Queen' and 'successful' to 'drink of water' and 'honeybee'. You may remember it most famously as the name of the Lioness in The Lion King.

data.title: Odin
data.definition: There's a lot of energy behind this name, taken from the Norse God of wisdom, poetry and war - also known as the 'Lord of Frenzy'! Whilst there's a dash of strength and power here, he was also known for his mental agility, so a good one for the extra smart pups!

data.title: Peach
data.definition: If your dog  is 'a real peach', this could make a really sweet girl's name and is of French, German and English origins.

data.title: Quest
data.definition: The name simply means a search or pursuit of something; but it certainly has a touch of the epicabout it! The name embodies a sense of adventure, curiosity, and a determined spirit.

data.title: Rex
data.definition: The name Rex carries a regal and powerful connotation, as it is derived from the Latin word for "king" or "ruler."  A classic name for dogs with a commanding presence and a natural air of authority.

data.title: Skye
data.definition: Named after the largest of Scotland's Inner Hebrides Islands, Skye is as footloose and free-wheeling a name as the expansive horizon it alludes to!

data.title: Thor
data.definition: The name "Thor" originates from Norse mythology and is associated with the Norse god of thunder and strength. It signifies power, bravery, and a commanding presence and would be a good name for large dog breeds, and those that command attention!

data.title: Ursa
data.definition: Ursa as a girl's name is of Scandinavian and Latin origin, Ursa is derived from the Latin name Orsa, and it means 'little she-bear'.

data.title: Wally
data.definition: Where's Wally? Let's hope you don't spend too much of your time asking about a little runaway! Wally has Germanic origins and means "ruler" or "powerful warrior", and might be a fun name choice for a pup who tends to camouflage amongst the outdoors, leaving you looking for them for the majority of the walk!

data.title: Xena
data.definition: The name "Xena" is often associated with the fictional character Xena, Warrior Princess, from the television series. It actually come from the Greek meaning 'guest' or 'stranger' - but what are strangers if not friends we just haven't met yet! Time to bring home your new best friend, and if you're looking for a name that's associated with strength, bravery, and a fierce warrior spirit, this might be the one for you!

data.title: Yoda
data.definition: The best-known character of all the Star Wars series and properties, Yoda is (see what we did there?). Yoda is the Jedi master who trains Luke Skywalker and is wise beyond his thousands of years—making this another solid choice for an older dog. Bringing home a puppy? Baby Yoda, from The Mandalorian is probably one of the cutest things we've ever seen and would totally work for a younger pup.

data.title: Zeus
data.definition: The name "Zeus" originates from Greek mythology and refers to the King of the Gods, God of the sky and thunder! Zeus was known for his power, strength, and authority so would make a good name for those strong-willed, confident pups ready to take on the world!

data.title: Zuzu
data.definition: Meaning "lily" in Czech and "little pearl" in Chinese, there's something a little fun and feisty about the name Zuzu!
